content: We can frame a layered two-tier self-improvement architecture for autonomous LLM agents where Self-Refine acts as the "tactical refinement engine" that polishes individual task outputs, while Reflexion acts as the "strategic memory layer" that accumulates generalized learning from repeated Self-Refine cycles to improve future task performance from the start. This architecture leverages the core strength of each approach: Self-Refine’s no-training, no-supervision fast iteration, and Reflexion’s dynamic long-term memory for cross-task self-reflection.
